key_insight: Sulfur vacancies in MoS2 do not diffuse independently; they self-organize into concentration-dependent clusters and networks that change the effective diffusion behavior.
engineering_relevance: Because vacancy mobility strongly depends on defect concentration, this mechanism helps explain and potentially tune memristive and transport behavior in MoS2 devices.
method: Kinetic Monte Carlo simulations of sulfur-vacancy dynamics
Transition rates parameterized from machine-learning interatomic potential molecular dynamics
Cluster analysis across different vacancy concentrations
evidence_signal: The abstract reports three distinct transport regimes and a concentration-dependent diffusion coefficient, with broad cluster-size distributions in the intermediate regime.
watch_item: The results are simulation-based and depend on the chosen ML potential and rate model, so quantitative transfer to specific devices may require experimental validation.
paper_title: Self-organized defect clustering and concentration-dependent vacancy diffusion in MoS$_2$
